data.title: Why Analysts See 2026 as a Breakout Year for Microsoft Stock (MSFT)
data.description:
As we head into 2026, the technology sector sits at a pivotal moment. The artificial intelligence (AI) revolution is clearly underway, yet investors remain divided. On one hand, this represents a genuine Fourth Industrial Revolution, with the U.S. firmly back in the lead on foundational technology for the first time in decades. On the other hand, the sheer scale of capital investment required to support enterprise and consumer AI has made some investors cautious.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

I believe Microsoft (MSFT) sits directly at the center of this transition—and 2026 is shaping up to be its sweet spot. As enterprises move from experimentation to full-scale AI deployment, Microsoft's combination of Azure, Copilot, and its massive installed base positions the company to be one of the biggest beneficiaries of the next phase of AI adoption. For that reason, I remain bullish on Microsoft and view it as one of the most compelling large-cap technology opportunities heading into 2026.

A Strong Year, With More to Come

Microsoft shares are up roughly 16% this year, broadly in line with the S&P 500 (SPX). While that performance may appear "average" on the surface, I believe it masks what is coming next. AI adoption across enterprises remains in its early stages. Many CIOs are only now formalizing long-term AI strategies. That shift—from pilots to production—is where Microsoft's platform strength becomes most visible.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Hold Steady After Christmas Holiday
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mostly flat Thursday night after markets reopened following the Christmas holiday. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were slightly up 0.09%, 0.02%, and 0.05%, respectively, at 8:44 p.m. EDT on December 25.

U.S. stock markets were closed on Thursday for Christmas Day after an early close on Wednesday, keeping trading activity muted. Volumes are expected to remain light ahead of a shortened New Year's week.

Earlier in the week, the S&P 500 closed at a record high, supported by stronger-than-expected U.S. economic growth in the third quarter.

As markets return to normal trading on Friday, investors will track upcoming economic data. With volumes thin, trading could remain calm, though late December often brings seasonal strength known as the "Santa Claus rally."

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: What's Next for UnitedHealth Stock (UNH) After the Recent Rebound? Analysts Weigh In
data.description:
Health insurer UnitedHealth Group (UNH) saw its shares rebound after a recent slide. The stock closed higher on Wednesday, ending a seven-session losing streak. The pullback came as investors reacted to regulatory reviews, Medicare Advantage billing scrutiny, and broader weakness across the healthcare sector. While these near-term issues remain in focus, most Wall Street analysts continue to take a constructive view on UnitedHealth's longer-term outlook, pointing to plans to improve margins and steady growth.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts Remain Positive on UnitedHealth 

Recently, J.P. Morgan analyst Lisa Gill has reiterated a Buy rating on UNH, saying the stock carries lower risk than many other managed care names. She expects earnings to improve as the company benefits from better pricing in its insurance business and gradual improvement at Optum, helping growth recover over time.

Gill also views UnitedHealth as one of the steadier names in managed care, pointing to its size and broad mix of businesses. She believes this gives the company more room to manage Medicare Advantage while working through challenges in Medicaid and ACA plans.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley believes UnitedHealth is taking firm steps to improve margins, even if that comes at the cost of slower membership growth. The firm pointed to benefit cuts, tighter pricing, and plans to shrink Medicare Advantage enrollment in 2026. Morgan Stanley expects Medicare Advantage margins to rise to about 2%–3% in 2026 and believes that a better rate outlook could support this recovery. 

Is UNH a Good Stock to Buy? 

Despite near-term challenges, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on UnitedHealth stock based on 17 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average UNH stock price target of $392.32 indicates about 20% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: PLTR vs. BBAI: Wall Street Weighs Which AI Stock Has More Upside in 2026
data.description:
As AI stocks continue to grab attention, investors are also looking at companies tied to government, defense, and enterprise AI spending. In this space, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and BigBear.ai (BBAI) have emerged as two closely watched names, each offering a different way to gain exposure to AI adoption. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we look at which stock analysts believe has more upside heading into 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Is Palantir Stock a Buy or Sell Now?

Palantir has become one of the most visible AI stocks over the past year, supported by steady demand from government agencies and rising interest from commercial customers. Shares have surged 158% year-to-date, driven in part by growing adoption of the company's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP). At the same time, long-term government contracts continue to provide a stable revenue base.

Analysts highlight improving margins, rising free cash flow, and expanding commercial revenue as key positives. Still, Wall Street remains cautious at current levels. Valuation is the main concern, with Palantir trading at a forward P/E of 268x, far above the sector average of about 25, suggesting much of the near-term growth may already be priced in.

That said, some analysts remain bullish on Palantir's longer-term outlook. For instance, top Bank of America analyst Mariana Perez Mora reiterated a Buy rating on Palantir and set a $255 price target, well above current levels. The analyst said Palantir's revenue growth shows no signs of slowing, fueled by strong demand from enterprise customers and expanding government contracts.

Is BigBear.ai a Good Stock to Buy Ahead of 2026?

BigBear.ai is a smaller and more speculative AI name, with a focus on defense, intelligence, and national security work. The company has benefited from rising interest in AI-driven defense tools as governments invest more in data analysis and decision-support systems. The stock has gained 49% year-to-date.

While revenue growth has improved, profitability remains a challenge. In the most recent quarter, BigBear.ai reported a net loss of $0.03 per share, narrower than the expected loss of $0.07. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $9.4 million, reflecting pressure from higher operating and SG&A costs.

From a valuation standpoint, BBAI trades at a price-to-sales multiple of about 13x, well above the sector median and its own historical average. However, it still trades below larger peers such as C3.ai (AI) and Palantir.

Wall Street analysts remain moderately bullish on BigBear.ai. They remain concerned about the execution risk due to the company's size and reliance on large government contracts, but believe that continued demand for defense-related AI work could support growth if the company executes well.

PLTR vs. BBAI: Which AI Stock Do Analysts Prefer?

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, analysts currently show a more favorable view on BigBear.ai, which carries a Moderate Buy rating and an average price target of $6.50, implying about 8% upside from current levels. Meanwhile, Palantir is rated Hold, with an average price target of $187.87, suggesting around 3% downside.

While both stocks have delivered strong gains this year — BigBear.ai up nearly 49% and Palantir up about 158% — Wall Street appears more cautious at current prices, especially after the sharp rallies.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Verizon (VZ) Eyes Bullish 2026 Following 'Reset Year'
data.description:

data.title: M&A News: Nvidia Agrees to Buy AI Chip Startup Groq for $20B
data.description:
(Updated)

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has agreed to acquire assets of AI chip startup Groq for $20 billion in cash, according to CNBC, citing Alex Davis, the CEO of Disruptive, which led Groq's most recent funding round. Davis said that the deal came together quickly, just months after Groq raised $750 million at a valuation of roughly $6.9 billion. Groq is expected to formally notify investors about the transaction soon, although its early-stage cloud business will not be included in the deal.

If completed, the acquisition would be Nvidia's largest by a wide margin. More specifically, the company's previous biggest deal was the $7 billion purchase of Mellanox in 2019. Importantly, Nvidia is well-positioned financially for this deal, as it ended October with $60.6 billion in cash and short-term investments. Groq, on the other hand, is targeting $500 million in revenue this year as demand for AI accelerator chips used in large language model inference surges.

The deal also fits into Nvidia's strategy of growing its influence across the AI ecosystem. In recent years, the company has increased investments in startups such as CoreWeave (CRWV), while also announcing plans to invest heavily in OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ) and Intel (INTC). Interestingly, the deal was framed as a "non-exclusive licensing agreement," and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang told CNBC, "While we are adding talented employees to our ranks and licensing Groq's IP, we are not acquiring Groq as a company."

What Is a Good Price for NVDA?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NVDA stock based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NVDA price target of $263.58 per share implies 39.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Five-Star Analyst Picks His Top 6 Chip Stocks for 2026
data.description:
The artificial intelligence boom is not slowing down and may still be in its early stages, according to five-star Bank of America (BAC) analyst Vivek Arya. While some investors are worried that valuations have become rich, Arya argues that the industry is only at the midpoint of a transformation that could last a decade. Furthermore, he sees Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) as the main leaders of this shift and believes that today's skepticism is similar to the doubts seen in past technology cycles that ultimately proved to be too conservative.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a result, Arya forecasted that global semiconductor sales will rise by about 30% year-over-year and exceed $1 trillion annually by 2026. In addition, he said that his preferred investments are companies with strong competitive advantages, which he identifies as those with high margins. Therefore, alongside Nvidia and Broadcom, he named Lam Research (LRCX), KLA (KLAC), Analog Devices (ADI), and Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) as his top large-cap semiconductor picks.

How Big is the AI Data Center Market?

Bank of America estimates that the total addressable market for AI data center systems could surpass $1.2 trillion by 2030. This equates to a 38% annual growth rate, with AI accelerators alone representing a $900 billion opportunity. Still, the market remains cautious due to the massive costs involved, as a single large data center can require more than $60 billion in capital spending. Nevertheless, Arya remains confident and argues that Big Tech must invest to defend its core businesses.

Which Chip Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the six chip stocks mentioned above, analysts think that Nvidia stock has the most room to run. In fact, Nvidia's average price target of $263.58 per share implies more than 40% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 3 Very Different Data Center Stocks to Watch in 2026
data.description:
The data center industry is growing very quickly as demand for artificial intelligence computing power continues to surge. As a result, companies like CoreWeave (CRWV), Equinix (EQIX), and Hut 8 (HUT) all stand to benefit from this trend, though they play very different roles in the data center market. Indeed, each company has a distinct business model and risk profile, which means that they don't provide the same type of AI exposure. Therefore, understanding these differences is important when deciding which stock fits a particular strategy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CoreWeave: The Pure-Play AI Specialist

To begin with, CoreWeave is the most direct, or "pure-play," bet on AI infrastructure. In fact, it operates specialized data centers built specifically for GPU-heavy AI workloads and works closely with Nvidia (NVDA) as well as AI labs like OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ) and Anthropic (PC:ANTPQ).

Unsurprisingly, the company is growing extremely fast, with a reported revenue backlog of $55.6 billion as of Q3 2025, but it is still unprofitable and carries a higher degree of risk. Nevertheless, its value proposition is its technical edge and specialized infrastructure that incumbents struggle to match.

Equinix: The World's Largest Data Center REIT

In contrast, Equinix sits at the opposite end of the spectrum. As the world's largest data center REIT, with more than 260 facilities globally, it focuses on colocation and interconnection for a broad customer base. Equinix's colocation allows companies to rent secure, high-tech space to house their computer servers instead of building their own expensive data centers.

Meanwhile, its interconnection services then act like a high-speed "private switchboard," letting those companies instantly and securely link their data to partners, customers, and clouds without using the public internet. While not purely an AI company, Equinix is adapting by supporting high-density AI workloads and offering distributed AI infrastructure.

Hut 8: The Crypto Miner Turned AI Contender

Lastly, Hut 8 falls somewhere in between as a transition story. Formerly known as a Bitcoin miner, the company has been shifting toward energy infrastructure and AI-focused data centers. More specifically, it recently signed a 15-year, $7 billion lease deal for a 245-megawatt AI data center project in Louisiana, which shows just how aggressively it is pivoting into AI.

It even has support from partners like Anthropic and financial backing from Google (GOOGL). However, with a much smaller market capitalization than Equinix, Hut 8 comes with more volatility.

Which Data Center Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three data center stocks mentioned above, analysts think that CRWV stock has the most room to run. In fact, CoreWeave's average price target of $133.29 per share implies more than 68% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
